title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for seeded simulation, input replay, state hashes, settings, and save migration and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reproducible replay and versioned local state. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.
Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Schema Migration
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 1940 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

content.text: Order work so Rng Streams has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Rng Streams, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Rng Streams precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Rng Streams, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 7213 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Rng Streams, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: One global seed guarantees replay after any rendering change.

options.text: Declare Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player outcome, revision, runtime, seed, input trace, accessibility profile, and frame, entity, collision, audio, and memory budgets for the quiz Rng Streams case

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Schema Migration
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Schema Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 5594 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Schema Migration.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Schema Migration, keep Schema Migration connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.
